text.title: Python Code Quality — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-code-quality application must obey.

rule.statement: Use Ruff as the single lint + format tool. Remove black, isort, flake8, autoflake, pyupgrade from dev dependencies.
rule.rationale: Ruff implements all five tools' rules in one Rust binary, 10–100x faster, with one config; coexistence creates conflicting opinions.

rule.statement: Run mypy or pyright in strict mode. Allow per-module override only for vendored or legacy code, never as the global default.
rule.rationale: Strict catches Optional misuse, implicit Any, untyped defs — the cheapest layer of defect detection. Global relaxation hides bugs.

rule.statement: Configure pytest-cov with a hard --cov-fail-under threshold (≥80% branches for new code).
rule.rationale: A threshold turns coverage into a gate, not a vanity number. Without it, coverage drifts down silently.

rule.statement: Run bandit (or ruff's S rules) on every commit. Block high-severity findings.
rule.rationale: SQL injection, hardcoded creds, weak hashes are easier to catch at commit time than in security review.

rule.statement: Make pre-commit the single canonical gate. CI re-runs the same hooks to prevent --no-verify bypass.
rule.rationale: Same checks locally and in CI → no merge-time surprises. Bypassing locally fails CI.

rule.statement: Set requires-python, ruff target-version, mypy python_version, and pyproject.toml [tool.ruff.target-version] all to the same Python version.
rule.rationale: Mismatched target versions produce false positives (e.g., 3.12 syntax flagged because ruff targets 3.10).
